title: Creating and Stopping Threads in Java
description: A flowchart illustrating the two main methods for creating threads and the recommended safe method for stopping them.
relationships: Main Program extends Thread to create a Thread Class Object
Main Program implements Runnable to create a Runnable Interface Object
Both object types are passed to a Thread constructor to create a Thread Instance
Calling start() on the instance begins execution as a Running Thread
To stop, the main program sets the Interrupt Flag on the running thread
The running thread checks its interrupted status and terminates cleanly to become a Stopped Thread
